summary: My aunt Crystal originally came from Yunnan, China. Yunnan is a province in the southwest part of China, and her hometown Lijiang is located in the northwest of Yunnan. She grew up there before moving to the United States in 1995 after marrying my uncle Ben. Yunnan has a rich cultural heritage, especially with ethnic groups like the Nakhi people, which we both belong to.

Lijiang is my aunt Crystal’s hometown, a prefecture-level city in northwest Yunnan. It’s famous for its Old Town, known for its beautiful waterways and bridges. My aunt owned a bar there and was a well-known singer in Lijiang. She met her husband there in 1993, and the Old Town is also a UNESCO World Heritage Site, which makes it a very special place for our family.

The Old Town of Lijiang, where my aunt Crystal lived and worked, is recognized as a UNESCO World Heritage Site. This designation highlights its unique system of waterways and historic bridges. It’s a place full of cultural significance and beauty, which made it a memorable backdrop for my aunt’s early life and career as a singer. The heritage status also attracts many visitors and preserves the traditions of the Nakhi people.

My aunt Crystal and I both belong to the Nakhi people, an ethnic group living in the foothills of the Himalayas in northwest China. The Nakhi culture is very important to us, and it’s reflected in my aunt’s singing and her work with the Seattle Ensemble of Songs and Dances. Through her performances, she helps promote our indigenous culture and arts overseas, keeping our traditions alive even far from home.
cues: Seattle Ensemble of Songs and Dances

summary: My aunt Crystal moved to the United States in 1995 after marrying my uncle Ben, who was an American colonel. They settled in Seattle, Washington, where they have lived for about 20 years now. Although she has a green card, my aunt prefers not to become a U.S. citizen because she frequently returns to China and wants to avoid visa issues. The U.S. has been home for our family, especially for my aunt’s daughter Cindy and granddaughter Taylor.

Seattle is where my aunt Crystal and uncle Ben settled after they got married. They have lived there for 20 years, and it’s where my aunt’s daughter Cindy grew up, graduated from Seattle Community College, and now works at an accounting firm. Seattle has become our family’s home, and my aunt is active there as a singer with the Seattle Ensemble of Songs and Dances, promoting Chinese indigenous culture.
cues: Seattle Ensemble of Songs and Dances

summary: My aunt Crystal’s eldest daughter Cindy attended Seattle Community College and graduated from there about 10 years ago. After finishing her studies, Cindy started working for an accounting firm in Seattle. The college played an important role in helping Cindy establish her career and settle into life in the United States, continuing our family’s journey here.
